cards.statement: Every skipped meal — did you notice?
cards.sub: Cats usually decrease food intake subtly when their body starts to feel unwell.
cards.alt: A fluffy cat resting near a soft lamp glow

cards.statement: They sit quietly — and you call it normal.
cards.sub: Chronic stress in cats is often mistaken for just temperament.
cards.alt: A tabby cat sitting in dramatic shadow and light

cards.statement: But something is watching silently — and caring.
cards.sub: PE detects small behavioral shifts that you might miss.
cards.alt: Cat silhouetted against a bright window

cards.sub: PE tracks sleep cycles, highlighting when rest is persistently disrupted.
cards.alt: A cat sleeping curled up under warm light

cards.statement: They hide in the darkest corner — you think they just want alone time.
cards.sub: Cats in pain often seek isolation instead of showing obvious distress.
cards.alt: A cat tucked into a shadowy corner of a room

cards.statement: The purring stopped — nobody noticed when.
cards.sub: A change in vocal habits can be one of the earliest silent signals.
cards.alt: Close-up of a quiet cat with soft, unreadable eyes

cards.statement: Less grooming, more matting — mistaken for laziness.
cards.sub: PE flags drops in self-care behavior that often go unnoticed for weeks.
cards.alt: A cat with slightly unkempt fur lying still

cards.statement: They stopped jumping to their favorite spot — and you just moved the cushion lower.
cards.sub: Reduced mobility is frequently dismissed as aging, not discomfort.
cards.alt: An empty windowsill where a cat used to perch

cards.statement: Water bowl untouched for hours — routine, or a red flag?
cards.sub: PE monitors hydration patterns to catch subtle shifts before they escalate.
cards.alt: A still water bowl in soft morning light

cards.statement: The litter box tells a story — most people never read it.
cards.sub: Changes in frequency or behavior around the litter box often precede visible symptoms.
cards.alt: A litter box in a quiet, dim corner of a home
